body:
Hold on to Your Wallet No Images? [Click here](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-e-jtilkjil-kujkulhluu-jy/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtilkjil-kujkulhluu-r/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtilkjil-kujkulhluu-y/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtilkjil-kujkulhluu-j/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtilkjil-kujkulhluu-t/)
### [Hold on to Your Wallet](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtilkjil-kujkulhluu-i/)
There are a whole bunch of Democrats who would like to be "the chosen one" to run against President Trump in the next election. Elizabeth Warren of Massachusetts, Tulsi Gabbard of Hawaii, and Julian Castro of Texas are a few who have already thrown their hats into the ring. I would expect a slew of others to jump in in the upcoming weeks and months. [Read this entry >>](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtilkjil-kujkulhluu-d/) _Previous Weekly Posts_ [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtilkjil-kujkulhluu-h/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtilkjil-kujkulhluu-k/)
### [Is "A Wall an Immorality"?](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtilkjil-kujkulhluu-u/)
Last week, Nancy Pelosi, who is once again the most powerful Member of the U.S. House of Representatives, claimed that "a wall is an immorality." Is she right? In my opinion, absolutely not. [Read this entry >>](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtilkjil-kujkulhluu-o/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtilkjil-kujkulhluu-b/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtilkjil-kujkulhluu-n/)
### [My Predictions for 2019](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtilkjil-kujkulhluu-p/)
Well, it's that time of year again. Time for me to go out on a limb, and predict what's going to happen in the upcoming year. Here goes. [Read this entry >>](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtilkjil-kujkulhluu-x/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtilkjil-kujkulhluu-m/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtilkjil-kujkulhluu-c/)
### [A Shutdown, Troop Withdrawals, and a Resignation](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtilkjil-kujkulhluu-q/)
First of all, I hope you and your loved ones had a very blessed Christmas. As we should always remember at this time of year, there are those in uniform and otherwise serving our nation in remote areas around the globe, who cannot be with their families, and may God keep them contented and protected until they are back home with their loved ones. [Read this entry >>](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtilkjil-kujkulhluu-a/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtilkjil-kujkulhluu-f/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtilkjil-kujkulhluu-z/)
### [Another Shutdown?](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtilkjil-kujkulhluu-v/)
Is the government really going to shut down in a couple days? And if so who's to blame? And will Social Security checks still go out? And will our soldiers facing hostile forces like the Taliban in Afghanistan still get paid? And if it snows, will my street still get cleared? WHAT'S GOING TO HAPPEN? [Read this entry >>](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtilkjil-kujkulhluu-e/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtilkjil-kujkulhluu-s/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtilkjil-kujkulhluu-g/)
### [Google - One Reason Republicans May Have a Harder Time Getting Their Message Out](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtilkjil-kujkulhluu-w/)
We had an interesting hearing in the House Judiciary Committee yesterday. The witness at the hearing was the CEO of GOOGLE - one of the most important companies in the world today. The CEO was Sundar Pichai, not necessarily a household name, but a very important mover and shaker nonetheless on the world scene today. name: Jay Inslee
subject: Don't let Wheeler DESTROY our Earth!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jay Inslee for Washington
body:
Don't give him more power to cause disaster.

| [](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7a489/2f5ea88d/794ff330/2cbdf828/2064973959/VEsH/ "https://action.jayinslee.com/page/s/wheeler-epa?utm_source=ema_2019-01-16_wheeler-epa-petition&source=ema_2019-01-16_wheeler-epa-petition&utm_campaign=wheeler-epa-petition&utm_medium=ema") Friend, Today, a Senate committee began to decide the fate of Trump's latest and largest affront to America's fight against climate change. The confirmation hearing on Trump's appointment of Andrew Wheeler to head the EPA is another major test for us. We've known for months that Wheeler is a terrible choice -- he's a coal lobbyist. Wheeler's built his career around dismantling our environmental progress. And in the months since taking the role of acting chief of the EPA, he's doubled down on that work. If confirmed, he will have even more power to chip away at our fight against climate change. So I'm calling on the Senate committee today not to confirm him. It's simple: A coal lobbyist should _not_ run the EPA. [**Join me in telling the Senate: Don't confirm a Big Coal shill like Andrew Wheeler to the EPA!**](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7a489/2f5ea88d/794ff330/2cbdf828/2064973959/VEsE/ "https://action.jayinslee.com/page/s/wheeler-epa?utm_source=ema_2019-01-16_wheeler-epa-petition&source=ema_2019-01-16_wheeler-epa-petition&utm_campaign=wheeler-epa-petition&utm_medium=ema") Since Wheeler began serving as acting chief of the EPA, he has proposed de-regulating power plants' coal ash production and slashing regulations for fuel standards in new cars. It's crystal clear that he is in the pocket of fossil fuel corporations -- and Trump clearly sees that as a positive. Our climate is in a state of crisis. Even if Trump doesn't care that he is setting our world up for disaster, we can fight this with everything we've got. [**Tell the Senate committee: Don't let Wheeler keep destroying our environment!**](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7a489/2f5ea88d/794ff330/2cbdf828/2064973959/VEsF/ "https://action.jayinslee.com/page/s/wheeler-epa?utm_source=ema_2019-01-16_wheeler-epa-petition&source=ema_2019-01-16_wheeler-epa-petition&utm_campaign=wheeler-epa-petition&utm_medium=ema") We can't let it end this way. Very truly yours, Jay Inslee | [SIGN ON »](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7a489/2f5ea88d/794ff330/2cbdf828/2064973959/VEsC/ "https://action.jayinslee.com/page/s/wheeler-epa?utm_source=ema_2019-01-16_wheeler-epa-petition&source=ema_2019-01-16_wheeler-epa-petition&utm_campaign=wheeler-epa-petition&utm_medium=ema")

---
Peter,

Here in Texas, the work never stops. **For as optimistic as we were during the 2018 election, we were never going to flip the state in one election cycle.** To build a political infrastructure, to turn non-voters into frequent voters, to make the case that the Democratic Party will make a real difference in people's lives: this is something we need to be doing every day, every month, every year, not just on the eve of an election. **With that in mind, even though the 2020 elections are a long way off, we are launching a program of "Appreciation and Action**." [Kicking off this Sunday in Austin](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/4282368/33872531/-167917559?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82MzkwNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiOGVmZWRiMy01NTFhLWU5MTEtOWYyYS0yODE4NzgzOTFlZmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=RnnEvRn4e-Sy4Vtmy0P8lXDzbJw0GhCkCEOjOROdvWE=), we will spend the weeks and months ahead traveling the Texas 10th Congressional District: to honor the individuals and organizations who dedicated themselves to political change during the 2018 election, and to launch a campaign of political action that will unite Democrats across our nine counties. **Appreciation comes first, because so many of you took extraordinary action last year, efforts we cannot take for granted**. From super volunteers to national organizations, from Democratic clubs to activist groups and labor unions, I am personally indebted to so many of you, and I know that countless Texans send their thanks. In 2018 we built a strong foundation for the work to come. **And yet, every time we come together, we need to advance the movement for progressive change**. In many parts of the Texas 10th, there is very little political organizing outside of the electoral cycle. But this month the Texas Legislature begins its five-month session, and major policies are at stake including public education, voting rights, and healthcare. Within TX-10 we have five Democrats in the Texas House, and all five are strong women leaders pushing important legislative initiatives. **We plan to use our resources and network to support our state representatives. And through this process we will strengthen our habits of political organizing.** Appreciation and Action begins Sunday at 2:00 p.m. at our old Austin campaign office, 5460 Guadalupe, Suite L135. **(And who knows, we might make another announcement that day.)** We hope to see you there! And if you can't make it, [you can follow online](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/4282369/33872532/729498802?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82MzkwNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiOGVmZWRiMy01NTFhLWU5MTEtOWYyYS0yODE4NzgzOTFlZmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=RnnEvRn4e-Sy4Vtmy0P8lXDzbJw0GhCkCEOjOROdvWE=).

With love and respect,
Mike

name: Abigail Spanberger
subject: Now we need to dig in
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Spanberger for Congress
body:
The Cook Political Report recently issued their first ratings for the 2020 House races, and once again, we are a "Toss Up". It wasn't long ago that we were celebrating such news; "Toss Up" status was an indicator that because of 16 months of work driven by passion and belief, we could win in what, just a year prior, had been a "Solid Republican" seat.
| | Dear Peter,

_The Cook Political Report_ recently issued their first ratings for the 2020 House races, and once again, we are a "Toss Up". It wasn't long ago that we were celebrating such news; "Toss Up" status was an indicator that because of 16 months of work driven by passion and belief, we could win in what, just a year prior, had been a "Solid Republican" seat.

However, our "Toss Up" status now means that we are one of the top targeted Democratic seats in the country. We will need to work just as hard, if not harder, to keep our seat in 2020 as we did to win it in 2018.

**We are launching our Campaign Reboot Fund, Peter, and we need your help.[Can you contribute today and help us ensure we're prepared to defend this seat in 2020?](https://act.myngp.com/el/FqhE6ZSlju6p2hvNVZ_gU_hXlKjnRyoHO5m7FbMA0Bw=/R1FGnM4hMpTAbAiIkvYGdhca7UqwAVNp3rPz9C3fQWc=?refcode=em_fr_2019.01.17_b1_as_all&amount=25)**

When I was sworn into office on January 3rd, I took an oath to protect and defend the Constitution of the United States. Now more than ever, America needs public servants who are guided by this oath and by a service-first focus, not by ambition or party agenda. That's the type of representation I bring to the people of Virginia's 7th District, and with [**your support**](https://act.myngp.com/el/FqhE6ZSlju6p2hvNVZ_gU_hXlKjnRyoHO5m7FbMA0Bw=/R1FGnM4hMpTAbAiIkvYGdhca7UqwAVNp3rPz9C3fQWc=?refcode=em_fr_2019.01.17_b1_as_all&amount=25), I will continue doing so.

The 2018 election was supposed to be "unwinnable" for us, and look where we are today. And it only happened because people like you stepped up and made it happen.

name: Ben & Jerry
subject: The Scoop on Bernie 2020
body:
# [](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7a675/bed43eb/829d3819/75fbab11/3407675921/VEsH/ "https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/s/run-bernie-run-ben-and-jerry?source=email-header-OR")
Friends,
Yes, there are several potential presidential candidates whose policy proposals make a lot of sense but when it comes down to it, we are STILL YEARNING FOR BERNIE!
He's the only one who has been consistent in his principles for the last 37 years in public office; the only one who has long term experience in both the House and the Senate, so he knows what he's up against; and the only one who can build on a deep existing base of tens of millions of supporters like us. Like you.
We're still yearning for Bernie because he's still yearning to end wealth and income inequality.
And we're yearning for Bernie because we know he can get Big Money out of politics and create a system that works for all of us by electing a president who refuses to be bought out by special interests and corporate lobbyists.
[_**Sign this petition and be a part of convincing Bernie Sanders to run for President.**_](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7a675/bed43eb/829d3819/75fbab10/3407675921/VEsE/ "https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/s/run-bernie-run-ben-and-jerry/?source=em190117-a")
We're supporting him because the next President of the United States will decide the fate of this country and our planet, and we believe Bernie is the best person for the job.
[_**Add your name to our petition now and be a part of convincing Bernie to run for president.**_](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7a675/bed43eb/829d3819/75fbab13/3407675921/VEsF/ "http://go.ourrevolution.com/page/s/run-bernie-run-ben-and-jerry/?source=em190117-a")
In solidarity,
Ben Cohen and Jerry Greenfield

name: Bernie Sanders
subject: #UTLAStrong
body:
[1]Bernie Sanders

Sisters and Brothers -

There is something happening in Los Angeles that you need to know about
and that we all need to do something about.

Today, for the first time in 30 years, more than 30,000 Los Angeles public
school teachers are on strike fighting for smaller class sizes and decent
wages, for nurses, counselors and librarians in their schools, and against
a coordinated effort from billionaires on the right to make money
privatizing public education.

Public education is fundamental to any functioning democracy, and teaching
is one of its most valuable and indispensable professions.

So how is it that the top 25 hedge fund managers in this country make more
money than the combined salaries of every kindergarten teacher?

How is it that the billionaires of this country get huge tax breaks, but
our teachers and children get broken chairs, flooded classrooms and
inadequate support staff in their schools?

That is what a rigged economy looks like.

In the richest country in the history of the world, our teachers should be
the best-paid in the developed world, not among the worst-paid.

So I stand in solidarity with the United Teachers of Los Angeles. Because
a nation that does not educate its children properly will fail, and I
applaud these teachers for leading this country in the fight to change our
national priorities. Today, I am asking you to do the same:

[ https://act.berniesanders.com/signup/UTLA_strike/?source=em190117-full&t=1001&akid=428%2E150750%2EYJAif- ]Add Your Name: Tell the striking teachers in Los Angeles that you are
following their struggle and stand in solidarity with them. We will make
sure your messages of support get to these teachers.

But what we really need in this country is a revolution in public
education.

What we accept as normal today with regards to education, I want your
grandchildren to tell you that you were crazy to accept.

And in my view, that conversation starts, but does not end, with
early-childhood education.

That is not just my opinion. Research tells us that the “most efficient
means to boost the productivity of the workforce 15 to 20 years down the
road is to invest in today’s youngest children.”

So it is not a radical idea to say that we need to provide free, full-day,
high-quality child care for every child, starting at age three, so that
they will be guaranteed a pre-kindergarten education regardless of family
income.

That is common sense.

But in the twenty-first century, a public education system that goes from
early childhood education through high school is not good enough.

The world is changing, technology is changing, our economy is changing. If
we are to succeed in the highly competitive global economy and have the
best-educated workforce in the world, I believe that higher education in
America should be a right for all, not a privilege for the few.

That means that everyone, regardless of their station in life, should be
able to get all of the education they need.

Today in America, hundreds of thousands of bright young people who have
the desire and the ability to get a college education will not be able to
do so because their families lack the money. This is a tragedy for those
young people and their families, but it is also a tragedy for our nation.

Our mission must be to give hope to those young people. If every parent in
this country, every teacher in this country, and every student in this
country understands that if kids study hard and do well in school they
will be able to go to college, regardless of the income of their family,
that will have a radical impact on primary and secondary education in the
United States—and on the lives of millions of families.

That is what we can accomplish by making public colleges and universities
tuition-free, because every American, no matter his or her economic
status, should have the opportunity for a higher education. And, at the
same time, we must substantially lower student debt.

But getting there will take a political revolution in this country, and a
radical change in national priorities.

Instead of giving huge tax breaks to billionaires and profitable
corporations, we must create the best public educational system in the
country. Instead of major increases in military spending, we must invest
in our kids.

And today, the most important step in that direction starts with standing
in solidarity with the teachers in Los Angeles.

[ https://act.berniesanders.com/signup/UTLA_strike/?source=em190117-full&t=1002&akid=428%2E150750%2EYJAif- ]Add Your Name: Tell the striking teachers in Los Angeles that you are
following their struggle and stand in solidarity with them. We will make
sure your messages of support get to these teachers.

Through our support for these teachers, we have a chance to reaffirm our
support for quality public education and the right of all children to
receive the best education possible.

Thank you for standing with them.

In solidarity,

Bernie Sanders

[ https://act.berniesanders.com/signup/UTLA_strike/?source=em190117-full&t=1003&akid=428%2E150750%2EYJAif- ]ADD YOUR NAME

You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time:
http://act.berniesanders.com/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?akid=428%2E150750%2EYJAif-

subject: Dear Madame Speaker. . .
body:
Today, President Donald J. Trump sent a letter to Speaker of the House Nancy Pelosi. | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=00e4129665&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| The White House • January 17, 2019
---
| | |
# **Dear Madame Speaker...**

**Today, President Donald J. Trump** sent a letter to Speaker of the House Nancy Pelosi (D-CA), informing her that her planned taxpayer-funded travel abroad would be postponed until after the Federal Government is reopened.

**“We will reschedule”** her excursion when the Shutdown is over, he writes.

**The President hopes Speaker Pelosi** will remain in Washington, and he made clear that there is a standing invitation to continue negotiations at any time. If the Speaker chooses to leave the country, it would only guarantee that 800,000 American workers will continue to go without pay for at least another week—and possibly longer.

**President Trump explained** that Speaker Pelosi is still welcome to make her trip by flying commercial. His letter today simply directs that U.S. Military resources not go toward this international trip while Federal workers wait for Congress to negotiate with President Trump and reopen the Government.

**Since the Shutdown began** , the President has canceled planned travel and stayed in Washington, ready to make a deal. On the other side, Speaker Pelosi took a vacation to Hawaii days after the Shutdown began, and over 30 Democratic Members of Congress took a chartered flight to Puerto Rico for a retreat with lobbyists and corporate executives.

[See President Trump’s full letter to Speaker of the House Nancy Pelosi.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=0c71db98a2&e=3ee4985eea)
